sourceMovie.title: Ice Age: Collision Course
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Ice Age: Collision Course for the same reason it broke through in 2016: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a animation rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Mike Thurmeier keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The adventure and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: A Shaun the Sheep Movie: Farmageddon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: A Shaun the Sheep Movie: Farmageddon (2019) is the natural next step after Ice Age: Collision Course: same adventure DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and animation elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Ice Age: Dawn of the Dinosaurs
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Ice Age: Collision Course who respond to its adventure will find Ice Age: Dawn of the Dinosaurs (2009) immediately familiar. Both films share a animation sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Ice Age: Collision Course fans keep landing on Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa (2008) — the adventure is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Over the Hedge
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Over the Hedge draws from the same adventure well as Ice Age: Collision Course, channeling animation in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2006 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The Croods: A New Age
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Croods: A New Age (2020) captures the adventure appeal of Ice Age: Collision Course without simply imitating it. The animation streak running through both films is what puts The Croods: A New Age at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Rio 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The adventure appeal of Ice Age: Collision Course — that funny edge, the animation — translates cleanly into Rio 2 (2014). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Open Season
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Open Season (2006) clearly understood what makes adventure work — the same quality that keeps Ice Age: Collision Course rewatchable. Expect the same funny rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Ice Age Adventures of Buck Wild
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Ice Age Adventures of Buck Wild (2022) is the kind of film Ice Age: Collision Course fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The adventure overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Strange World
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Strange World shares Ice Age: Collision Course's gift for adventure — the 2022 film delivers animation without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Planes: Fire & Rescue
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Ice Age: Collision Course compelling — the adventure, the animation — shows up in Planes: Fire & Rescue (2014) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that funny storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ice Age: Collision Course?
faq.answer: Try A Shaun the Sheep Movie: Farmageddon, Ice Age: Dawn of the Dinosaurs, Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa—each shares DNA with Ice Age: Collision Course while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ice Age: Collision Course?
faq.answer: Ice Age: Collision Course (2016) is best described as Adventure, Animation, Family, Comedy, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ice Age: Collision Course part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Ice Age Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
